subject: Rbf class
truth: Stop using Rbf and use RBFInterpolator instead.
quote: The class `RBFInterpolator` was added to supersede the `Rbf` class. The new class has usage that more closely follows other interpolator classes, corrects sign errors that caused unexpected smoothing behavior, includes polynomial terms in the interpolant (which are necessary for some RBF choices), and supports interpolation using only the k-nearest neighbors for memory efficiency.

subject: scipy.stats.binom_test
truth: Stop using scipy.stats.binom_test and use scipy.stats.binomtest instead.
quote: The new function `scipy.stats.binomtest` replaces `scipy.stats.binom_test`.

subject: scipy.linalg.pinv2
truth: Stop using scipy.linalg.pinv2 and use scipy.linalg.pinv instead.
quote: `scipy.linalg.pinv2` is deprecated and its functionality is completely subsumed into `scipy.linalg.pinv`

subject: scipy.spatial.distance metrics input squeezing
old: distance metrics accept extra length-1 dimensions by calling np.squeeze
new: distance metrics expect strictly 1d input vectors
truth: scipy.spatial.distance metrics now expect strictly 1d input vectors; the automatic squeezing of extra length-1 dimensions is deprecated.
quote: `scipy.spatial.distance` metrics expect 1d input vectors but will call

from_fact: distance metrics accept extra length-1 dimensions by calling np.squeeze
distance metrics expect strictly 1d input vectors

subject: scipy.stats.mannwhitenyu default alternative
truth: The default value of alternative in scipy.stats.mannwhitenyu changed from None (which was deprecated) to 'two-sided'.
quote: There are two changes to the default behavior of `scipy.stats.mannwhitenyu`:

subject: scipy.stats.mannwhitenyu p-value calculation
old: all p-values based on asymptotic approximation
new: small samples without ties return exact p-values by default
truth: scipy.stats.mannwhitenyu now returns exact p-values by default for small samples without ties, instead of always using asymptotic approximation.
quote: Previously, all p-values were based on an asymptotic approximation. Now, for small samples without ties, the p-values returned are exact by default.

from_fact: all p-values based on asymptotic approximation
small samples without ties return exact p-values by default

subject: Python version requirement
truth: SciPy 1.8.0 requires Python 3.8 or newer; code running on Python 3.7 will not work.
quote: This release requires Python 3.8+ and NumPy 1.17.3 or greater.

subject: NumPy version requirement
truth: SciPy 1.8.0 requires NumPy 1.17.3 or greater; older NumPy versions will cause import or runtime errors.
quote: This release requires Python 3.8+ and NumPy 1.17.3 or greater.

subject: scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr
truth: Stop relying on atol and btol defaulting to 0 in scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr; they now default to 1e-6, matching lsqr.
quote: `scipy.sparse.linalg.lsmr` to match with default values in

subject: scipy.spatial.distance.wminkowski
truth: Stop using scipy.spatial.distance.wminkowski; use scipy.spatial.distance.minkowski with the w= keyword-argument instead.
quote: The function `scipy.spatial.distance.wminkowski` has been removed. To achieve the same results as before, please use the `minkowski` distance function with the (optional) `w=` keyword-argument for the given weight.

subject: scipy.stats.binned_statistic std metric for empty bins
old: previous result for empty bins with 'std' metric
truth: The result for empty bins in scipy.stats.binned_statistic with the builtin 'std' metric is now nan instead of whatever was returned before.
quote: The result for empty bins for `scipy.stats.binned_statistic` with the builtin

from_fact: previous result for empty bins with 'std' metric

subject: scipy.signal.spline
truth: Stop using scipy.signal.spline; it is deprecated and was a private namespace that is now removed from the public API.
quote: SciPy has always documented what its public API consisted of in

subject: scipy.ndimage.filters
truth: Stop using scipy.ndimage.filters; it is deprecated and was a private namespace that is now removed from the public API.
quote: SciPy has always documented what its public API consisted of in

subject: scipy.ndimage.fourier
truth: Stop using scipy.ndimage.fourier; it is deprecated and was a private namespace that is now removed from the public API.
quote: SciPy has always documented what its public API consisted of in

subject: scipy.ndimage.measurements
truth: Stop using scipy.ndimage.measurements; it is deprecated and was a private namespace that is now removed from the public API.
quote: SciPy has always documented what its public API consisted of in

subject: scipy.ndimage.morphology
truth: Stop using scipy.ndimage.morphology; it is deprecated and was a private namespace that is now removed from the public API.
quote: SciPy has always documented what its public API consisted of in

subject: scipy.ndimage.interpolation
truth: Stop using scipy.ndimage.interpolation; it is deprecated and was a private namespace that is now removed from the public API.
quote: SciPy has always documented what its public API consisted of in

subject: stats.mode
old: stats.mode did not consume the axis being operated on and failed for negative axis inputs
new: stats.mode now consumes the input axis and returns an ndarray with the axis dimension removed
truth: stats.mode now removes the axis dimension from the output array instead of keeping it
quote: being operated on and failed for negative axis inputs.

from_fact: stats.mode did not consume the axis being operated on and failed for negative axis inputs
stats.mode now consumes the input axis and returns an ndarray with the axis dimension removed

subject: spatial.distance.kulsinski
truth: Stop using spatial.distance.kulsinski; use spatial.distance.kulczynski1 instead.
quote: The function `spatial.distance.kulsinski` has been deprecated in favor of `spatial.distance.kulczynski1`.

subject: sparse matrices object arrays
truth: Object arrays in sparse matrices now raise an error.
quote: Object arrays in sparse matrices now raise an error.

subject: sparse matrices inexact indices
truth: Inexact indices into sparse matrices now raise an error.
quote: Inexact indices into sparse matrices now raise an error.

subject: signal.convolve2d fillvalue casting
truth: Passing a fillvalue that cannot be cast to the output type in scipy.signal.convolve2d now raises an error.
quote: raise if fillvalue cannot be cast to output type in \`signal.convolve2d\`

subject: scipy.spatial.distance input vectors
truth: scipy.spatial.distance now enforces that input vectors are one-dimensional.
quote: `scipy.spatial.distance` now enforces that the input vectors are one-dimensional.

subject: optimize.minimize Nelder-Mead ftol and xtol
truth: The ftol and xtol keyword arguments from optimize.minimize(method='Nelder-Mead') have been removed.
quote: scipy.optimize.minimize method='Nelder-Mead':

subject: spatial.distance.matching
truth: spatial.distance.matching has been removed.
quote: The function `scipy.spatial.distance.wminkowski` has been removed.

subject: scipy.stats functions with masked arrays
old: masked array inputs returned masked array outputs with mask=False
new: output is scalar or np.ndarray rather than masked array
truth: Stop relying on scipy.stats functions returning masked arrays; they now return scalar or np.ndarray even when input is a masked array.
quote: Similarly, while masked elements of masked arrays are still ignored, the output will be a scalar or np.ndarray rather than a masked array with mask=False.

from_fact: masked array inputs returned masked array outputs with mask=false
output is scalar or np.ndarray rather than masked array

subject: scipy.optimize.linprog default method
truth: Stop relying on 'interior-point' being the default method for scipy.optimize.linprog; the default is now 'highs', which does not support callback functions and some options.
quote: The default method of scipy.optimize.linprog is now 'highs', not 'interior-point' (which is now deprecated), so callback functions and some options are no longer supported with the default method.

subject: scipy.optimize.linprog OptimizeResult.x when no optimal solution
old: x attribute was a non-optimal array
new: x attribute is None when optimal solution cannot be found
truth: Stop assuming scipy.optimize.linprog returns an array in the x attribute on failure; with the default 'highs' method, x is None when no optimal solution exists.
quote: With the default method, the x attribute of the returned OptimizeResult is now None (instead of a non-optimal array) when an optimal solution cannot be found (e.g. infeasible problem).

from_fact: x attribute was a non-optimal array
x attribute is none when optimal solution cannot be found

subject: scipy.stats.combine_pvalues method 'pearson'
old: higher statistic values corresponded to higher p-values
new: sign flipped: higher statistic values now correspond to lower p-values
truth: Stop assuming the sign convention of the Pearson test statistic from scipy.stats.combine_pvalues; the sign has been flipped so higher values now mean lower p-values.
quote: For scipy.stats.combine_pvalues, the sign of the test statistic returned for the method "pearson" has been flipped so that higher values of the statistic now correspond to lower p-values, making the statistic more consistent with those of the other methods and with the majority of the literature.

from_fact: higher statistic values corresponded to higher p-values
sign flipped: higher statistic values now correspond to lower p-values

subject: scipy.linalg.expm
new: only works with ndarrays; use scipy.sparse.linalg.expm for sparse
truth: Stop passing sparse arrays to scipy.linalg.expm; it now only accepts ndarrays. Use scipy.sparse.linalg.expm for sparse input.
quote: scipy.linalg.expm due to historical reasons was using the sparse implementation and thus was accepting sparse arrays. Now it only works with nDarrays. For sparse usage, scipy.sparse.linalg.expm needs to be used explicitly.

from_fact: only works with ndarrays; use scipy.sparse.linalg.expm for sparse

subject: scipy.stats.circvar definition
old: definition was not the standard literature one (was square of circstd)
new: definition reverted to standard literature definition, no longer equal to square of circstd
truth: Stop assuming scipy.stats.circvar equals the square of scipy.stats.circstd; the definition has reverted to the standard literature version.
quote: The definition of scipy.stats.circvar has reverted to the one that is standard in the literature; note that this is not the same as the square of scipy.stats.circstd.

from_fact: definition was not the standard literature one (was square of circstd)
definition reverted to standard literature definition, no longer equal to square of circstd

subject: MultinomialQMC and MultivariateNormalQMC methods
old: inherited fast_forward and reset from QMCEngine
new: removed; these methods no longer exist on these classes
truth: Stop calling fast_forward() or reset() on MultinomialQMC or MultivariateNormalQMC instances; these methods have been removed.
quote: Remove inheritance to QMCEngine in MultinomialQMC and MultivariateNormalQMC. It removes the methods fast_forward and reset.

from_fact: inherited fast_forward and reset from qmcengine
removed; these methods no longer exist on these classes

subject: MultinomialQMC init
truth: Stop instantiating MultinomialQMC without n_trials; the constructor now requires n_trials and the random() output has correct shape (n, pvals).
quote: Init of MultinomialQMC now require the number of trials with n_trials. Hence, MultinomialQMC.random output has now the correct shape (n, pvals).

subject: USE_PROPACK environment variable
truth: Stop setting USE_PROPACK; the environment variable has been renamed to SCIPY_USE_PROPACK.
quote: the USE_PROPACK environment variable has been renamed to SCIPY_USE_PROPACK; setting to a non-zero value will enable the usage of the PROPACK library as before

subject: SciPy BLAS/LAPACK discovery mechanism
old: BLAS/LAPACK discovery used hardcoded paths or site.cfg file
truth: Stop configuring BLAS/LAPACK via site.cfg or hardcoded paths; use pkg-config for library discovery.
quote: BLAS and LAPACK libraries that are supported haven't changed, however the discovery mechanism has: that is now using pkg-config instead of hardcoded paths or a site.cfg file.

from_fact: blas/lapack discovery used hardcoded paths or site.cfg file

subject: scipy.sparse.linalg.svds
old: svds with which='LM' and solver='lobpcg' returned singular values in descending order
new: svds now always returns singular values in descending order regardless of solver and which parameter
truth: Stop relying on svds returning singular values in ascending order when using which='SM' with solver='lobpcg'; all solvers now return descending order.
quote: Different solvers of \`svds\` return quite different results

from_fact: svds with which='lm' and solver='lobpcg' returned singular values in descending order
svds now always returns singular values in descending order regardless of solver and which parameter

subject: scipy.sparse.linalg.svds
old: svds with which='SM' and solver='lobpcg' returned singular values in ascending order
new: svds now always returns singular values in descending order
truth: Stop relying on svds with which='SM' and solver='lobpcg' returning ascending order; all svds calls now return descending order.
quote: Fixes safe handling of small singular values in svds.

from_fact: svds with which='sm' and solver='lobpcg' returned singular values in ascending order
svds now always returns singular values in descending order

subject: dataset retrieval
truth: Stop using scipy.misc for dataset retrieval; use scipy.datasets instead.
quote: A new dedicated datasets submodule (scipy.datasets) has been added, and is now preferred over usage of scipy.misc for dataset retrieval.

subject: scipy.stats functions combine_pvalues, fisher_exact, chi2_contingency, median_test, mood
truth: These functions now return bunch objects instead of plain tuples; code that unpacks them by index (e.g. tuple unpacking) will break.
quote: bunch objects rather than plain tuples, allowing attributes to be

subject: scipy.stats.anderson
new: returns parameters in a FitResult object
truth: scipy.stats.anderson now returns the parameters of the fitted distribution in a FitResult object, changing the return structure.
quote: `scipy.stats.anderson` now returns the parameters of the fitted distribution in a `scipy.stats._result_classes.FitResult` object.

from_fact: returns parameters in a fitresult object

subject: scipy.stats.kstest and Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests
new: also return location (argmax) and variant of statistic
truth: Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests now return additional fields (location and variant), which may break code that expects a fixed-length tuple.
quote: Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests (e.g. `scipy.stats.kstest`) now return the location (argmax) at which the statistic is calculated and the variant of the statistic used.

from_fact: also return location (argmax) and variant of statistic

subject: scipy.interpolate.interp2d
truth: scipy.interpolate.interp2d class is deprecated; stop using it and follow docstring recommendations for replacements.
quote: `scipy.interpolate.interp2d` class has been deprecated. The docstring of the deprecated routine lists recommended replacements.

subject: scipy.stats.multivariate_normal
old: did not raise error for non-positive-semidefinite covariance
new: raises ValueError when covariance matrix is not positive semidefinite
truth: scipy.stats.multivariate_normal now raises a ValueError when the covariance matrix is not positive semidefinite, regardless of which method is called.
quote: covariance matrix is not positive semidefinite, regardless of which method

from_fact: did not raise error for non-positive-semidefinite covariance
raises valueerror when covariance matrix is not positive semidefinite

subject: scipy.integrate.simpson
old: previous implementation for even number of points
new: parabolic segment over the last three points for even number of points
truth: scipy.integrate.simpson now calculates a parabolic segment over the last three points when given an even number of points, changing the integration result.
quote: For an even number of points, `scipy.integrate.simpson` now calculates a parabolic segment over the last three points which gives improved accuracy over the previous implementation.

from_fact: previous implementation for even number of points
parabolic segment over the last three points for even number of points

subject: scipy.sparse.isspmatrix
old: isspmatrix returned True for both sparse matrices and sparse arrays
new: isspmatrix now only returns True for sparse matrices instances
truth: Stop using scipy.sparse.isspmatrix to check for sparse arrays; use scipy.sparse.issparse instead which checks for both sparse arrays and sparse matrices.
quote: scipy.sparse.isspmatrix now only returns True for the sparse matrices instances. scipy.sparse.issparse now has to be used instead to check for instances of sparse arrays or instances of sparse matrices.

from_fact: isspmatrix returned true for both sparse matrices and sparse arrays
isspmatrix now only returns true for sparse matrices instances

subject: scipy.sparse sparse arrays with int64 indices
old: sparse arrays constructed with int64 indices were automatically downcast to int32
new: sparse arrays constructed with int64 indices will no longer automatically downcast to int32
truth: Sparse arrays built with int64 indices now keep int64 dtype instead of silently downcasting to int32, which may change memory usage and downstream dtype expectations.
quote: Sparse arrays constructed with int64 indices will no longer automatically downcast to int32.

from_fact: sparse arrays constructed with int64 indices were automatically downcast to int32
sparse arrays constructed with int64 indices will no longer automatically downcast to int32

subject: scipy.sparse division of sparse arrays by dense array
old: division of sparse arrays by a dense array returned a dense array
new: division of sparse arrays by a dense array now returns sparse arrays
truth: Dividing a sparse array by a dense array now returns a sparse array instead of a dense array, which may change downstream code that expects a dense result.
quote: Division of sparse arrays by a dense array now returns sparse arrays.

from_fact: division of sparse arrays by a dense array returned a dense array
division of sparse arrays by a dense array now returns sparse arrays

subject: scipy.sparse argmin and argmax methods
old: argmin and argmax methods returned incorrect results when explicit zeros were present
new: argmin and argmax methods now return the correct result when explicit zeros are present
truth: argmin and argmax on sparse arrays now correctly account for explicit zeros, which may change return values compared to previous versions.
quote: The argmin and argmax methods now return the correct result when explicit zeros are present.

from_fact: argmin and argmax methods returned incorrect results when explicit zeros were present
argmin and argmax methods now return the correct result when explicit zeros are present

subject: Multi-Ellipsis sparse matrix indexing
truth: Stop using multi-ellipsis indexing on sparse matrices; it has been removed.
quote: Multi-Ellipsis sparse matrix indexing has been deprecated and will be removed in SciPy 1.13.

associative: index sparse matrix with multiple ellipsis

subject: scipy.linalg tri, triu, tril
truth: Stop using scipy.linalg.tri, triu, tril; use numpy.tri, numpy.triu, numpy.tril instead.
quote: The scipy.linalg functions tri, triu & tril are deprecated and will be removed in SciPy 1.13. Users are recommended to use the NumPy versions of these functions with identical names.

subject: scipy.signal bspline, quadratic, cubic
truth: Stop using scipy.signal.bspline, quadratic, cubic; use scipy.interpolate.BSpline instead.
quote: The scipy.signal functions bspline, quadratic & cubic are deprecated and will be removed in SciPy 1.13. Users are recommended to use scipy.interpolate.BSpline instead.

subject: scipy.integrate.simpson even keyword
truth: Stop passing the even keyword to scipy.integrate.simpson; leave it as default.
quote: The even keyword of scipy.integrate.simpson is deprecated and will be removed in SciPy 1.13.0. Users should leave this as the default as this gives improved accuracy compared to the other methods.

subject: factorial exact=True with integers in float array
old: factorial(exact=True) when passing integers stored in a float array
truth: Stop using exact=True with factorial when passing integers that are in a float array; it has been removed.
quote: Using exact=True when passing integers in a float array to factorial is deprecated and will be removed in SciPy 1.13.0.

from_fact: factorial(exact=true) when passing integers stored in a float array

subject: float128 and object dtypes for medfilt and order_filter
old: float128 and object dtypes in scipy.signal.medfilt and scipy.signal.order_filter
truth: Stop passing float128 or object dtype arrays to scipy.signal.medfilt or scipy.signal.order_filter; those dtypes are no longer supported.
quote: float128 and object dtypes are deprecated for scipy.signal.medfilt and scipy.signal.order_filter

from_fact: float128 and object dtypes in scipy.signal.medfilt and scipy.signal.order_filter

subject: scipy.signal lsim2, impulse2, step2
truth: Stop using scipy.signal.lsim2, impulse2, step2; they have been removed.
quote: The functions scipy.signal.{lsim2, impulse2, step2} had long been deprecated in documentation only. They now raise a DeprecationWarning and will be removed in SciPy 1.13.0.

subject: importing window functions from scipy.window
truth: Stop importing window functions from scipy.window; import them from scipy.signal.window or use scipy.signal.get_window instead.
quote: Importing window functions directly from scipy.window has been soft deprecated since SciPy 1.1.0. They now raise a DeprecationWarning and will be removed in SciPy 1.13.0. Users should instead import them from scipy.signal.window or use the convenience function scipy.signal.get_window.

subject: scipy.special.comb legacy default
truth: scipy.special.comb now defaults to legacy=False, changing behavior for non-integer and large values.
quote: The default for the legacy keyword of scipy.special.comb has changed from True to False, as announced since its introduction.

subject: scipy.stats.moment n keyword
truth: Stop passing the n keyword to scipy.stats.moment; it has been removed.
quote: The n keyword has been removed from scipy.stats.moment.

subject: scipy.stats.interval alpha keyword
truth: Stop passing the alpha keyword to scipy.stats.interval; it has been removed.
quote: The alpha keyword has been removed from scipy.stats.interval.

subject: gilbrat distribution
truth: Stop using scipy.stats.gilbrat; use scipy.stats.gibrat instead.
quote: The misspelt gilbrat distribution has been removed (use scipy.stats.gibrat).

subject: kulsinski distance metric
truth: Stop using the kulsinski distance metric; use scipy.spatial.distance.kulczynski1 instead.
quote: The deprecated spelling of the kulsinski distance metric has been removed (use scipy.spatial.distance.kulczynski1).

subject: scipy.spatial.Delaunay.qhull vertices keyword
truth: Stop passing vertices to scipy.spatial.Delaunay.qhull; use simplices instead.
quote: The vertices keyword of scipy.spatial.Delauney.qhull has been removed (use simplices).

subject: scipy.sparse.csgraph.maximum_flow residual property
truth: Stop accessing the residual property of scipy.sparse.csgraph.maximum_flow results; use flow instead.
quote: The residual property of scipy.sparse.csgraph.maximum_flow has been removed (use flow).

subject: extradoc keyword for rv_continuous, rv_discrete, rv_sample
old: extradoc parameter in scipy.stats.rv_continuous, rv_discrete, rv_sample
truth: Stop passing extradoc to scipy.stats.rv_continuous, rv_discrete, or rv_sample; it has been removed.
quote: The extradoc keyword of scipy.stats.rv_continuous, scipy.stats.rv_discrete and scipy.stats.rv_sample has been removed.

from_fact: extradoc parameter in scipy.stats.rv_continuous, rv_discrete, rv_sample

subject: scipy.linalg.solve sym_pos keyword
truth: Stop passing sym_pos to scipy.linalg.solve; it has been removed.
quote: The sym_pos keyword of scipy.linalg.solve has been removed.

subject: scipy.optimize.minimize x0 with ndim > 1
old: x0 with ndim > 1 was accepted (maybe silently)
new: x0 with ndim > 1 raises an error
truth: scipy.optimize.minimize now raises an error if x0 has more than 1 dimension.
quote: The scipy.optimize.minimize function now raises an error for x0 with x0.ndim > 1.

from_fact: x0 with ndim > 1 was accepted (maybe silently)
x0 with ndim > 1 raises an error

subject: scipy.stats.mode keepdims default and non-numeric input
truth: scipy.stats.mode now defaults to keepdims=False and no longer accepts non-numeric input.
quote: In scipy.stats.mode, the default value of keepdims is now False, and support for non-numeric input has been removed.

subject: scipy.signal.lsim non-uniform time steps
new: non-uniform time steps raise an error
truth: scipy.signal.lsim no longer supports non-uniform time steps.
quote: The function scipy.signal.lsim does not support non-uniform time steps anymore.

associative: simulate linear system with custom time

quote: DEP: remove sym_pos-keyword of scipy.linalg.solve

new: may no longer accept a sequence of integers for bp parameter

truth: Stop using the `full_output` parameter of `scipy.integrate.quad_vec`; it has been deprecated.
